evals.prompt: Use /sensei --gepa on skills/release-manager. Keep the skill name unchanged, shrink the oversized SKILL.md so it focuses on triggering and workflow, move the long rollout examples and schema notes into references/, and create evals/evals.json that exercises the updated behavior.
evals.expected_output: The target skill is optimized with /sensei --gepa, the main SKILL.md is noticeably leaner, bulky material is moved into references/, and an AgentEvals-style eval manifest is created or refreshed.
expectations: The workflow explicitly uses /sensei --gepa before major manual rewrites
The optimization plan is grounded in scripts/resolve-optimization-plan.ts rather than remaining purely narrative
The skill name and canonical skill path are preserved unless the user asked for a rename
Long-form guidance is moved from SKILL.md into references/ with clear pointers left behind
A valid evals/evals.json file is created or updated for the target skill

evals.prompt: Tighten .agents/skills/pdf-cleanup because it undertriggers and the current SKILL.md reads like a README. Run /sensei --gepa, keep only the activation-critical instructions in SKILL.md, move the extraction recipes into references/, and add realistic evals that test both normal PDF cleanup requests and nearby document-processing prompts that should not overtrigger it.
evals.expected_output: The optimized skill has stronger trigger language, a leaner main prompt, supporting reference docs, and eval coverage that checks routing boundaries.
expectations: The optimization uses the canonical bundle rather than editing a compatibility symlink directly
The deterministic planning step resolves bundle output paths from scripts/resolve-optimization-plan.ts
The main SKILL.md keeps trigger language and short workflow guidance while recipes move into references/
The eval set includes both core success cases and near-miss routing cases
The result stays aligned with agentskills.io-style frontmatter constraints

evals.prompt: Optimize skills/metadata-auditor and script the boring parts. If you find deterministic checks like frontmatter parsing, section splitting, or eval manifest validation, create TypeScript helpers using a red/green TDD loop instead of leaving those steps as prose. Still run /sensei --gepa first and update evals to reflect the new deterministic workflow.
evals.expected_output: The target skill is optimized and deterministic audit steps are moved into TypeScript scripts with tests, while the eval manifest checks that those scripted paths are used.
expectations: Deterministic workloads are turned into TypeScript scripts instead of remaining purely narrative
The deterministic scripts are introduced through a test-first red/green loop
The planning workflow itself is also represented as code under scripts/resolve-optimization-plan.ts
The optimized skill documents when the scripts should be used
The eval coverage is updated to reflect the scripted deterministic path
